steps.title: Identify what Henry's finding must logically suggest
steps.content: The passage states the standard economic view was that liberalization "would make it easier for companies to raise money from investors." Henry then found that companies in 11 countries received "significant increases in investment in the three years following liberalization." The blank must complete the phrase "suggesting that ______" — so the answer must be the conclusion Henry's data supports. Since his empirical finding matches what economists predicted, the suggestion is that the data confirmed the standard view.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
The data showed investment increased after liberalization — exactly what economists predicted — so the correct answer aligns those two facts.
  • Choice A — 'empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus about the consequences of liberalization.': correctly states that Henry's finding (increased investment) matched the economists' prediction (easier to raise money), i.e., the data confirmed the consensus. ✓
  • Choice B — 'economists who held the standard view of liberalization failed to anticipate some serious negative effects of liberalization.': introduces negative effects that Henry's data — which showed investment increases — does not support. ✗
  • Choice C — 'companies did not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurred.': contradicts the passage, which says increases happened "in the three years following," not after at least three years. ✗
  • Choice D — 'companies in Malaysia experienced a greater increase in investment following liberalization than did companies in the Philippines.': makes a country-specific comparison that Henry's averaged data does not establish. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
